evals.name: BR/EDR: DM Packet Types for FEC Reliability
evals.prompt: Create a BR/EDR connection between a central and peripheral at 15m distance. The environment is noisy so I need forward error correction on the data packets. evals.prompt: Simulate two BLE piconets 1m apart vertically. Both use ConnectionInterval=0.04, ActivePeriod=0.015, PHYMode=LE1M, each with 1 central + 1 peripheral sending 100 Kbps (PacketSize=50). Tests the core GAP-only workflow: 1 Central, 4 Peripherals, undirected advertising, KPIs

context.setup: Requires Bluetooth Toolbox and Wireless Network Toolbox

evals.name: GAP Connection: GAP-Only Multi-Peripheral
evals.prompt: Simulate a BLE network where 1 Central establishes connections with 4 Peripherals using the GAP connection establishment procedure. Use undirected advertising. Add On-Off traffic from central to all peripherals at 100 Kbps with 50-byte packets. Run for 2 seconds and report LL throughput and PLR for each connection.

assertions.description: Sets GAPConfig via property assignment (node.GAPConfig = config), not a function call
Does NOT use configureConnection (GAP-only mode means automatic scheduling)

assertions.expected: DataRate value is a reasonable Kbps number (e.g., 100), not a bps number (e.g., 100000 or 100e3)
assertions.description: DataRate is in Kbps (value <= 1000), not bps
Generated script runs without error and produces KPIs
Calls addNodes to register nodes with the simulator
Calls run() to execute the simulation
GAP config assigned on BOTH central AND peripherals (not just central)
assertions.expected: Both central.GAPConfig and peripheral.GAPConfig property assignments present
AdvertisingInterval= specified in peripheral GAP configuration
assertions.description: AdvertisingInterval set on peripheral GAP config
Does NOT use configureConnection in GAP-only mode (automatic scheduling)

evals.description: Tests the combined GAP + explicit connection config mode with cross-layer constraints

Different AccessAddress string for each configureConnection call

ScanInterval value is large enough to accommodate ScanWindow plus the total of all ActivePeriod values
DataRate value is a reasonable Kbps number (e.g., 100), not a bps number (e.g., 100000 or 100e3)
assertions.description: DataRate is in Kbps (value <= 1000), not bps
